Notes: Led P1 and P3. Had some ravens flying around right behind me while belaying Tom on P2. Lives up to the hype! Last climb in Yosemite for this trip :')

Up at 5:30, climbing by 8. I took the first four pitches linked into two 200 footers, then hung out in a heinous hanging belay for an hour and a half while Matt and Tom worked on a snafu with the haul bag. I did the first half of the next pitch, but came down for Matt to tackle the free section ahead. I got to do the challenging C2 part, though! Matt took the next block of two, then Tom the last three in the dark with my headlamp. Topped out just before midnight, I barely squeaked in my Duolingo with four minutes to spare! Rapped along the fixed lines after stashing supplies for Matt and Toms push on Triple-D. Walked back to Camp 4 and in bed by 4am. Saw so many tree frogs after dark on the rock, some mice, a softball sized fluffy rodent, and some mountain quail near the base! 22 hours camp to camp.
First ledge poop ever! Went quite well. Got to do a wild lower-out from under the roof. Rapping down from the top went so fast!! Todd and Forrest are total Gs
Pre-fixed the rope on P5 while Matt and Tom were setting up the portaledge below the roof. Forgot the dang tagline so I had to back-aid it as well! What an awesome line!

Partner(s): Tom Cr., Matt O’., Anna Re., Zoey Sp.
Notes: Bit of a pump fest, crux right off the ground.

What. A. Fight. Definitely one of my proudest onsights to date! An absolute fight, struggled on the crux at the start which didn't bode well, but I hung in there and bagged it! Kept perfect pace with the sun the whole climb, my feet were in the shade but the rest of me in the sun :skull-emoji
Nuthin' to say
Found a copes gray tree frog mid pitch 1 and hung out with a praying mantis at the belay atop P1. Almost got nailed by a football sized rock while belaying Tom on P2!
Always wanted to do this line just because I see it from the start of the via all the timeeee
Sick setting! Very worried about loose rock the whole time though
Aid practice for El Cap. A creative lead!

What an awesome line for pitch 2! Its only sad that its so short. Took an adventurous way to the base of the money pitch. Pulled some death blocks down and got yelled at by a bat in a crack on pitch 2. WILD!
Super bad conditions. The lichen was still very soggy. Still so fun though!
Nuthin' to say
Fell after using a glassy smear instead of a bomber foot jam. Lameee

W/ Tom Cronin. Led the crux roof, fuckin gnarly!
